scenarios.title: Feature Development
scenarios.description: Building a new REST API endpoint with tests in an existing monorepo

summary: Cursor does not remove developer judgment. It compresses context gathering, boilerplate, test generation, debugging loops, and review prep so engineers can spend more time on higher-quality design decisions.

before.label: Traditional developer workflow

steps.action: Search the codebase for existing patterns
Understand how similar endpoints are structured
Write boilerplate for route, handler, and types
Write unit and integration tests manually
Fix lint and type errors across files
Open PR and address review comments

painPoints: Heavy time spent on context gathering before writing code
Boilerplate and test scaffolding are repetitive
Debugging loops and review prep add hidden cycle time

after.label: Cursor-assisted agentic workflow

steps.action: Agent reads ticket and pulls relevant codebase context
Plan Mode creates an implementation plan across files
Developer reviews and approves the plan
Agent edits endpoint, types, and tests together
Terminal runs the test suite inside Cursor
Cursor explains failures and proposes targeted fixes
Developer reviews diff and refines edge cases
PR opened with stronger context for reviewers

improvements: Plan-first workflow keeps the developer in control of scope
Multi-file edits reduce manual boilerplate and test setup
Faster debug loops with codebase-aware failure analysis
